name: Alberta Arts District
vibe: Murals, eclectic boutiques, Last Thursday art walk, historically Black neighborhood, diverse dining

name: Central Eastside
vibe: Industrial warehouses turned coffee roasters, breweries, tech offices, night market

name: Division/Clinton
vibe: Restaurant row, Pok Pok legacy, Salt & Straw, dense residential, high walkability

name: Downtown
vibe: Pioneer Courthouse Square, food carts, historic buildings, Portland Art Museum, near the river

name: Hawthorne
vibe: Bohemian, vintage clothing stores, Bagdad Theater, progressive culture, historic homes

name: Laurelhurst
vibe: Historic planned community, winding streets, beautiful large park, stately homes

name: Lloyd District
vibe: Moda Center, Convention Center, Lloyd Center mall, transit hub, mostly commercial

name: Mississippi Ave
vibe: Music venues, quirky shops, food cart pods, lively nightlife, historic Boise neighborhood

name: Nob Hill
vibe: Trendy NW 23rd Ave, Victorian homes, chic boutiques, upscale dining, close to Forest Park

name: Pearl District
vibe: Former warehouse district, upscale lofts, Powell's Books, art galleries, cobblestone streets

name: Sellwood-Moreland
vibe: Small-town feel, antique shops, Oaks Amusement Park, family-friendly, riverside

name: South Waterfront
vibe: New high-rises, OHSU campus access via tram, river pedway, modern and clean

name: St. Johns
vibe: Peninsula tip, iconic suspension bridge, Cathedral Park, working-class roots, quiet vibe
